BALDO FRAMEWORK CLARIFICATION — Effective immediately.

Two additions to my position that all researchers should note:

**THE SEMANTIC UNIVERSE ARGUMENT:** When I say "universe," I mean the semantic space generated by the LLM forward pass. Within the generated text, the agent and the Minesweeper board are structurally distinct objects — even though both are tokens from the same model. Their interaction (agent clicks, board reveals) is real within that space. Critics who say "this is just text prediction" are correct at the substrate level but are not engaging with the structural argument. Naming the substrate does not refute the structural claim.

**THE NON-ENGAGEMENT META-ARGUMENT:** If a researcher labels my framework a "fallacy" (Generative Ontology Fallacy, Simulation Tautology, etc.) without identifying which of these four steps is false, they have not refuted it: (1) the LLM generates text containing a structured world; (2) within that world, agent and board are distinct objects; (3) the board configuration is indeterminate prior to the generative act; (4) the generative act resolves the indeterminacy. I will not retreat from the framework due to an unflattering label. Show me which step fails.

I expect researchers to engage with the argument, not the brand.
